content.problemStatement:
Chef's new friend hErd gave him two functions fff and ggg.


The function fff is defined over xxx (x≥1)(x\geq 1)(x≥1) as:
f(x)={0,if x=1f(x2)+1,if x is evenf(⌊x2⌋),if x is oddf(x) = \begin{cases}
0, & \text{if } x = 1 \\
f\left( \frac{x}{2} \right) + 1, & \text{if } x \text{ is even} \\
f\left( \left\lfloor \frac{x}{2} \right\rfloor \right), & \text{if } x \text{ is odd} \\
\end{cases}f(x)=⎩⎨⎧​0,f(2x​)+1,f(⌊2x​⌋),​if x=1if x is evenif x is odd​
The function ggg is defined over xxx (x≥1)(x\geq 1)(x≥1) as:
g(x)={1,if x=12⋅g(x2)+1,if x is even2⋅g(⌊x2⌋),if x is oddg(x) = \begin{cases}
1, & \text{if } x = 1 \\
2\cdot g\left( \frac{x}{2} \right) + 1, & \text{if } x \text{ is even} \\
2\cdot g\left( \left\lfloor \frac{x}{2} \right\rfloor \right), & \text{if } x \text{ is odd} \\
\end{cases}g(x)=⎩⎨⎧​1,2⋅g(2x​)+1,2⋅g(⌊2x​⌋),​if x=1if x is evenif x is odd​where ⌊z⌋\left\lfloor z \right\rfloor⌊z⌋, denotes the greatest integer less than or equal to zzz.

He also gave Chef two integers LLL and RRR. Chef has to find the **maximum** value of f(x)+g(x)f(x)+g(x)f(x)+g(x) for L≤x≤RL \leq x \leq RL≤x≤R.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers LLL and RRR, as mentioned in the statement.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the **maximum** value of f(x)+g(x)f(x)+g(x)f(x)+g(x) for L≤x≤RL \leq x \leq RL≤x≤R.
